author: @Suzuki_Hiakura
text:
With that closing statement, one YouTuber made a generator out of some exercise machine that required one of the kids to be pulling the handle to power the Wii, and when one kid was tired or it was their turn to bowl, they swapped quickly and had to keep the pace. Think it was just the Wii plugged into the generator instead of the wall, but still. 

When I first saw a hamster-wheel crane, it was in a video covering some architect's class or whatnot near France. They are building a castle using traditional methods and have either spent or will have to spend approximately 20 years to make it. The last video I saw was covering how they built a container for storing the grain and were working to build the church portion (forget the actual name). 

I still want to make a human-powered generator that I can get on every so often to exercise and power my home or charge my batteries lol

author: @mikestrain4747
text: I am very tempted to make one of these for my 14 year old and set it up as the only power source for his computer. I think I would call it a "round-to-it" that way when I ask him to do something and he says he will do it when he gets around to it I can point out that I already got him a round-to-it lol I also think it might help him remember to take more frequent breaks.

author: @kevinmcculloch5541
text:
I can't wait for the 7 foot serpentine coil and a thousand neodymium magnets arranged north, south, north, south......

Thankyou for all the effort you put in to this channel, it is one of my favorites.

replies.author: @TnTOmnibus
replies.text: i am sooo tempted lol - cheers mte

replies.author: @garycheatham9472
replies.text: I was thinking same thing but I'm not into exercising more a feather turbine or a rose for the ladies

author: @colleenforrest7936
text: I'm trying to figure out how long it would take to 3d print all the sections you'd need to build a life sized hampster wheel 🤔

replies.author: @TnTOmnibus
replies.text: i like the challenge! but a while lol

replies.author: @Vibe77Guy
replies.text: I'm wondering if I could make a template, cut the ball windmill blades from 55 gallon plastic drums, and then heat weld them together into a larger ball windmill. Then run more or less standard generators from the rim support wheels.

author: @grendel1960a
text: did you know that in your initial shot from Canterbury Cathedral you were just about 100m from a treadmill crane, its 2/3 of the way up the tower

replies.author: @TnTOmnibus
replies.text: it wasn't there when i shot it mate - there can be months between shots and video !

replies.author: @grendel1960a
replies.text: @TnTOmnibus its not visible its inside the tower, in a part most visitors cant get access to, I went in there once for work (my company were doing a wiring test and inspect and I had to measure up for the drawings for the report so got access everywhere) it was a donkey treadmill and is built into the tower (so they cant get it out as the beams are too big to go down the stairwell) the crane part is long gone, if a donkey died they had to get a new one up there, so the donkey lived up there until the cathedral was finished. I do have a not very good picture though, but it was taken in a dimly lit room, with a digital camera when they were very new (it was a kodak and took pictures to a floppy disk)

replies.author: @grendel1960a
replies.text: ps, the clock mechanism for the striking of the clock in the shorter tower is also interesting and a gorgeous sight.

author: @Vibe77Guy
text:
Awsome idea to omnibus so many projects!

A "ball windmill" of that size would make impressive Kinetic Sculpture  yard art.

replies.author: @TnTOmnibus
replies.text: yes it would and be fun to watch
